Petition To Instruct Co-Trustees To Sell Trust Property; Or To Distribute Trust Property; For Breach Of Trust; To Reduce Trustee Compensation; And To Preclude Use Of Trust Funds
Off Calendar Sep-11-2023 Continued To Jan-22-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

The following matters were heard: PTR-23-306399 The Richard C. Blum and Dianne Feinstein Joint Property Revocable Trust and PTR-23-306442 The 1996 Dianne Feinstein Trust.

Appearances: John Hartog for and with Katherine Feinstein (petitioner) by zoom.
Adam Pines appeared by zoom for Annette Blum, Eileen Blum and Heidi Blum (remainder beneficiaries).
Steven Braccini and Alexandra Banis for Michael Kline, Marc Scholvinck and Verett Mims (respondents/trustees).

Judge Picquet informed the parties that he has been assigned to hear all of these related matters including the third case (306509) that is not on calendar today. The court also made it clear to everyone that there are to be no recordings of these proceedings. In addition, the court noted that a request for dismissal of the Amended Petition to Confirm Trustee in case number 306422 has been filed. Mr. Hartog stated that he will be filing a new petition based on the issues raised in the response and confirmed that there is no active petition on that case. Further discussion was held regarding the issue of whether the parties are willing to go to mediation, the court's authority to order mediation under Breslin, the setting of a trial conference, Mr. Hartog's request that the court order the trustees to sell the Seadrift property and pay all of the expenses of making the property ready for sale, and deposit all of the remaining proceeds into an interest bearing account that can only be withdrawn by order of the court or agreement of the parties, the request that the trustees provide accountings, the accounting issue not being before the court today, there being no evidence that the sale of the Stinson Beach property should happen today/no evidentiary points, the matter needing to be adjudicated by way of a trial, the terms of the trust, the requirement of the marital deduction, the obligation of the trustees to make the unproductive property productive and carry out their fiduciary duties, the property being desirable four months out of the year, the issue of leasing the property rather than renting, other options that may be available to make the property productive, whether the trustees have the ability to make interim distributions, the estate tax liability not being known yet, the creditors/debts not being known, there being no estate tax required to fund the marital gift, there being other properties in the trust that are worth more than Stinson Beach, etc. The court stated that it is looking for a global outcome and orders the parties to mediation. The parties indicated that they will do private mediation and that they have already selected someone. Mediation is to be completed by December 11, 2023. The parties are to notify the Probate Examiner as to the outcome of mediation by December 18, 2023. The Petition to Instruct Co-Trustees to Sell Trust Property, etc. is continued to and the parties are to return on January 22, 2024 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The parties agree that all of the related cases including 306509 are subject to the mediation order. The court orders the parties to give the best accounting/a good faith interim accounting and strongly encourages counsel to have the accountings provided for mediation purposes. The accounting is to be provided, but not filed. The hearing date of October 11, 2023 in case number 306509 is also continued to January 22, 2024. The court will issue a case management orders ("CMO"). The parties acknowledge that the CMO will also apply to all of the related cases including the new petition that is yet to be filed. A mediation order and a case management order will be issued upon filing of the new petition regarding case number 306442.

Judge: Roger T. Picquet. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: A. Aguerre. Not reported.
